cases.title: OrganizerAdmin configures full EventRuleConfig on draft event via PATCH API
preconditions: Draft event exists with default EventRuleConfig
OrganizerAdmin JWT with scope over eventId
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig payload: capacity, waitlistEnabled, registrationOpenAt/registrationCloseAt, checkinOpenAt/checkinCloseAt, feedbackRequired, feedbackOpenAt/feedbackCloseAt
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} with OrganizerAdmin Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 200 on PATCH; GET 200 reflects all submitted ruleConfig fields; organizer updates event-level business configuration through standard REST API without database scripts or deployment steps

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin updates event capacity during Draft phase
preconditions: Draft event with EventRuleConfig capacity 50
OrganizerAdmin JWT authorized for eventId
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig.capacity 100
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} and inspect ruleConfig.capacity and version
cases.expected: PATCH 200; capacity updated to 100 in persisted EventRuleConfig; change applied through application API only, satisfying NFR-14 operability for capacity configuration per FR-02

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin toggles waitlist and feedback policy without technical intervention
preconditions: Draft event with waitlistEnabled false and feedbackRequired false
OrganizerAdmin JWT with event scope
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig waitlistEnabled true and feedbackRequired true
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} with admin token
cases.expected: PATCH 200; ruleConfig.waitlistEnabled and ruleConfig.feedbackRequired both true in GET response; business policy fields updated via standard organizer API per workflow A EventRuleConfig configuration step

cases.title: EventRuleConfig version increments on each critical config update
preconditions: Draft event with EventRuleConfig version 1
OrganizerAdmin actor context
Postgres database running per docker-compose local runtime
steps: Invoke eventService.update with ruleConfig capacity change
Invoke second update changing registrationOpenAt
Read event_rule_configs row for eventId in Postgres
cases.expected: version increments from 1 to 2 then 3 on successive updates; configuration version history trackable without manual schema intervention per event_rule_configs.version constraint

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin completes multi-section event setup form without technical steps
preconditions: Web app running with OrganizerAdmin authenticated
New or draft event edit flow available per forms UX event setup sections 1-5
steps: Navigate to event create or edit flow
Complete Section 1 basic details, Section 2 capacity/waitlist, Section 3 registration window, Section 4 check-in window, Section 5 feedback/certificate settings
Submit form and reopen event detail or edit view
cases.expected: All configured values persist and display on reload; organizer configures EventRuleConfig through guided UI with progressive disclosure, no SQL, config files, or developer tooling required

cases.title: Updated waitlist policy takes effect on subsequent registrations
preconditions: Published event RegistrationOpen at capacity with waitlistEnabled false
OrganizerAdmin JWT
Participant JWT available for new registration attempt
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} enabling waitlistEnabled with reasonCode and reasonText per post-open governance
POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Participant Authorization bearer
cases.expected: Registration returns Waitlisted state instead of Rejected; config change applied through API propagates to registration behavior without service restart or manual data fix

cases.title: EventRuleConfig persisted in Postgres via application service not ad-hoc SQL
preconditions: Draft event with OrganizerAdmin actor
Postgres database running per docker-compose local runtime
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig update through API handler
Query event_rule_configs table for eventId
Confirm no direct SQL migration or seed script was required for the update
cases.expected: event_rule_configs row updated with new field values and incremented version; maintainability requirement met because organizers mutate config through application layer backed by Postgres, not manual DBA intervention

cases.title: Cross-field window validator returns actionable error for conflicting ruleConfig times
preconditions: Event rule config validator module available
Draft event ruleConfig with checkinCloseAt before checkinOpenAt
steps: Call validateEventRuleConfig or equivalent with conflicting check-in window timestamps
Inspect returned error code and message
cases.expected: Validation fails with deterministic error and user-safe message explaining how to fix window order; supports low-setup-error organizer UX per design overview without requiring technical support to diagnose invalid config

cases.title: Post-registration-open rule config update remains self-service for OrganizerAdmin with audit trail
preconditions: Event in RegistrationOpen state
OrganizerAdmin JWT with mandatory reasonCode and reasonText payload
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig capacity increase, reasonCode, and reasonText
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?entityType=EventRuleConfig with admin token
cases.expected: PATCH 200; config update succeeds without developer intervention; audit log entry event.rule_config.updated persisted with before/after diff; governance guardrails do not block operable admin self-service per testing plan Flow C

cases.title: OrganizerStaff blocked from event rule config PATCH
preconditions: Draft event with OrganizerStaff JWT assigned to eventId
Valid ruleConfig mutation payload
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig capacity change using staff Authorization bearer
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} and verify ruleConfig unchanged
cases.expected: HTTP 403 with error code EVENT_RULE_CHANGE_FORBIDDEN; EventRuleConfig unchanged; only OrganizerAdmin may change event-level rules per BR-21

cases.title: Participant blocked from event rule config PATCH
preconditions: Published event with valid Participant JWT registered for the event
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig mutation using Participant Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 403 EVENT_RULE_CHANGE_FORBIDDEN; no EventRuleConfig mutation; config update remains admin-only operation

cases.title: Post-open critical rule config change rejected without mandatory reason
preconditions: Event in RegistrationOpen state
OrganizerAdmin JWT
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig capacity change omitting reasonCode and reasonText
cases.expected: HTTP 422 with AUDIT_REQUIRED_FOR_CRITICAL_CHANGE or equivalent; config not updated; post-open changes require explicit reason so misconfiguration risk R-02 is mitigated while preserving governed self-service updates

cases.title: Capacity reduction below current Registered count is rejected
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event with capacity 20 and 15 Registered participants
OrganizerAdmin actor with reason payload if post-open
steps: Attempt PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} setting ruleConfig.capacity to 10
Verify event_rule_configs.capacity remains 20 in Postgres
cases.expected: HTTP 422 semantic rule violation with user-safe message; capacity not reduced below active Registered count; prevents misconfigured capacity per BR-03 while allowing safe config edits through standard API

cases.title: Event setup form preserves entered values after cross-field validation failure
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin on event setup form Section 4 check-in window
Other sections already filled with valid data
steps: Enter checkinCloseAt earlier than checkinOpenAt
Submit form
Observe inline or page-level validation feedback without navigation away
cases.expected: Validation error displayed with how-to-resolve guidance; previously entered field values retained; immediate fail feedback per NFR-13 without losing organizer input or requiring technical recovery

cases.title: assertEventRuleConfigChangePermission rejects non-admin roles
preconditions: Authorization helper assertEventRuleConfigChangePermission or equivalent available
Actor context with role OrganizerStaff or Participant
steps: Call permission guard with OrganizerStaff actor attempting ruleConfig mutation
Call permission guard with Participant actor attempting ruleConfig mutation
cases.expected: Both invocations throw EVENT_RULE_CHANGE_FORBIDDEN before domain mutation; config change pipeline enforces BR-21 at validation layer step per validation rules catalog
